message.content:
Alright, imagine you have a big toy factory in a town called Detroit. For a long time, this factory makes lots of shiny cars, and many people come to work there. This makes the town very happy and busy; everyone has enough pennies to buy candy and keep their houses nice.

But then, a few things happen. First, other towns far away start making cars too, and they make them cheaper. Also, some people in charge of the toy factory don't take good care of it, and it starts to get old and not work so well. So, fewer people want to buy toys from there.

Because of this, some people lose their jobs. When there are fewer jobs, people don't have as many pennies to spend. Some people move away to find new jobs in other towns. Without the shiny toy factory running as it used to, the town becomes less busy and a bit sad.

When there are fewer people and less money, it's hard to keep everything nice and neat, like keeping the parks clean or fixing broken lights. Some places become empty, and that can sometimes make other problems, like people not feeling safe.

So, that's why Detroit isn't as shiny as it once was. But there are still people there who are trying to make it better bit by bit!
